description_long: This is a compact biplane with a robust fuselage and two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically, connected by a series of struts. The aircraft features a prominent front-mounted propeller and a visible engine cowling, suggesting a classic piston-engine design. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail layout. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel, characteristic of a taildragger configuration. The cockpit is positioned near the front of the fuselage and appears to be open, with no visible canopy or enclosure. The entire exterior is finished in a uniform dark color, giving the airplane a sleek, monolithic appearance.
description: A dark, compact biplane with stacked wings, a front propeller, and an open cockpit, featuring a classic taildragger landing gear and a conventional tail.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the stacked biplane wings, exposed engine details, and the front propeller.
description_view_1: The front view shows the dual wings with supporting struts, the central propeller, and the open cockpit behind it.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the biplane structure, tail assembly, and propeller.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the tail section, including the single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, as well as the biplane wing arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts; no winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles visible.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine and propeller, likely single-seat.
